title: Atylosia trinervia
intro: Atylosia trinervia, commonly known as the three-veined siris or three-veined acacia, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Atylosia trinervia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Atylosia trinervia is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
